sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Initiate an intra-era hard fork on Cardano mainnet to Protocol Version 11.0, named the 'van Rossem' hard fork.
Stated amount: not stated in document

Deliverables: Upgrade Cardano mainnet protocol from version 10.0 to 11.0 while remaining in the Conway era., Make new Plutus primitives from CIP-0109, CIP-0132, CIP-0133, CIP-0138, and CIP-0153 available., Make all Plutus built-in functions consistently available across Plutus V1, V2, and V3., Add UPLC case expressions for Bool, Integer, and Data., Enforce VRF key hash uniqueness at the ledger level and promote or revise several ledger validation predicates., Improve PPView mismatch reporting in Cardano Node.
Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposed hard fork upgrades Cardano mainnet to protocol major version 11 and minor version 0 while remaining in the Conway era.
Claim (technical, independently_verifiable, high materiality): The upgrade adds new Plutus primitives defined in CIP-0109, CIP-0132, CIP-0133, CIP-0138, and CIP-0153.
Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): All Plutus built-in functions, including newly introduced ones, will be available across Plutus V1, V2, and V3 after the hard fork.
Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): UPLC case expressions over Bool, Integer, and Data will provide significant performance improvements and cleaner script logic.
Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The hard fork preserves Protocol Version 10 functionality and leaves transaction shape unchanged, reducing ecosystem upgrade effort.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Testing reports demonstrate no behavioral regressions, complete specification-to-implementation conformance for new ledger rules, and correct operation of new Plutus primitives and case expressions across Plutus V1, V2, and V3.
missing: Independent evidence for: UPLC case expressions over Bool, Integer, and Data will provide significant performance improvements and cleaner script logic.
Independent evidence for: The hard fork preserves Protocol Version 10 functionality and leaves transaction shape unchanged, reducing ecosystem upgrade effort.

Independent evidence for: Testing reports demonstrate no behavioral regressions, complete specification-to-implementation conformance for new ledger rules, and correct operation of new Plutus primitives and case expressions across Plutus V1, V2, and V3.
Independent evidence for: Security audits have been undertaken for Plutus BLS primitives and execution costs for all Plutus primitives.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposed hard fork upgrades Cardano mainnet to protocol major version 11 and minor version 0 while remaining in the Conway era." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "UPLC case expressions over Bool, Integer, and Data will provide significant performance improvements and cleaner script logic." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
